name: USS Johnnie Hutchins (DE 360)
events: 10 Jun 1945 At 0852 H/10, USS Icefish (T/Cdr. R.W. Peterson, USN) arrived at Subic Bay where she moored alongside USS Anthedon (T/Capt. R.E. Hawes, USN). USS Icefish was escorted in by USS Johnnie Hutchins (Lt.Cdr. H.M. Godsey, USNR) by which had joined around 0430 H/10.
The survivors were landed and USS Icefish fuelled. Also some minor repairs were made. (1)

11 Jun 1945 Around 0800 H/11, USS Icefish (T/Cdr. R.W. Peterson, USN) departed Subic Bay to resume her 4th war patrol but now in the Gulf of Siam. She was escorted out until 1145 H/11 by USS Johnnie Hutchins (Lt.Cdr. H.M. Godsey, USNR). (1)
18 Jun 1945 USS Hawkbill (T/Cdr. W. Scanland, Jr., USN) ended her fourth war patrol at Subic Bay, Philippines.
She was escorted in USS Johnnie Hutchins (Lt.Cdr. H.M. Godsey, USNR) by which had joined around 0500 I/18.
USS Hawkbill was then taken in hand for refit by USS Anthedon (T/Capt. R.E. Hawes, USN).
8 Jul 1945 USS Carbonero (Cdr. C.L. Murphy, USN) ended her 1st war patrol at Subic Bay, Philippines. She was escorted in by USS Johnnie Hutchins (Lt.Cdr. H.M. Godsey, USNR).
At Subic Bay she was refitted by USS Howard W. Gilmore and a relief crew of Submarine Division 222.
20 Jul 1945 USS Blueback (Cdr. M.K. Clementson, USN) ended her 3rd war patrol at Subic Bay, Philippines. She was escorted in by USS Johnnie Hutchins (Lt.Cdr. H.M. Godsey, USNR).
